label: 278s Manufacturing USA

Coordination With Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership Centers, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. H, title XCIV, § 9415134 Stat. 4822Jan. 1, 2021“Notwithstanding section 34(d)(2)(A)(iv) of the National Institute for [of] Standards and Technology Act (), each Manufacturing USA Institute (established under subsection (d) of such section) shall, as appropriate, contract with a Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership Center (established under section 25 of such Act []) in each State in which such Institute provides services, either directly or through another such Center, to provide defense industrial base-related outreach, technical assistance, workforce development, and technology transfer assistance to small and medium-sized manufacturers. No Center shall charge in excess of its standard rate for such services. Funds received by a Center through such a contract shall not constitute financial assistance under section 25(e) of such Act.”15 U.S.C. 278s(d)(2)(A)(iv)15 U.S.C. 278k

Expansion of Manufacturing USA Network, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–92, div. A, title XVII, § 1741(b)133 Stat. 1837Dec. 20, 2019“Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary of Commerce shall take such actions as may be necessary to increase the number of Manufacturing USA institutes that participate in the Manufacturing USA Network.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. B, title VII, § 702128 Stat. 2220Dec. 16, 2014“Congress finds the following:“(1)In 2012, manufacturers contributed $2.03 trillion to the economy, or ⅛ of United States Gross Domestic Product.“(2)For every $1.00 spent in manufacturing, another $1.32 is added to the economy, the highest multiplier effect of any economic sector.“(3)Manufacturing supports an estimated 17,400,000 jobs in the United States—about 1 in 6 private-sector jobs. More than 12,000,000 Americans (or 9 percent of the workforce) are employed directly in manufacturing.“(4)In 2012, the average manufacturing worker in the United States earned $77,505 annually, including pay and benefits. The average worker in all industries earned $62,063.“(5)Taken alone, manufacturing in the United States would be the 8th largest economy in the world.“(6)Manufacturers in the United States perform two-thirds of all private-sector research and development in the United States, driving more innovation than any other sector.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. B, title VII, § 702128 Stat. 2220Dec. 16, 2014“Congress finds the following:“(1)In 2012, manufacturers contributed $2.03 trillion to the economy, or ⅛ of United States Gross Domestic Product.“(2)For every $1.00 spent in manufacturing, another $1.32 is added to the economy, the highest multiplier effect of any economic sector.“(3)Manufacturing supports an estimated 17,400,000 jobs in the United States—about 1 in 6 private-sector jobs. More than 12,000,000 Americans (or 9 percent of the workforce) are employed directly in manufacturing.“(4)In 2012, the average manufacturing worker in the United States earned $77,505 annually, including pay and benefits. The average worker in all industries earned $62,063.“(5)Taken alone, manufacturing in the United States would be the 8th largest economy in the world.“(6)Manufacturers in the United States perform two-thirds of all private-sector research and development in the United States, driving more innovation than any other sector.”

children.heading: Agency head
children.label: 1 Agency head
children.text: The term “agency head” means the head of any Executive agency (as defined in ), other than the Department of Defense.section 105 of title 5

children.heading: Regional innovation initiative
children.label: 2 Regional innovation initiative
children.text: The term “regional innovation initiative” has the meaning given such term in .section 3722(f)(1) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of Manufacturing USA Program
children.label: b Establishment of Manufacturing USA Program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Institute a program to be known as the “Manufacturing United States of America Program” or the “Manufacturing USA Program” (referred to in this section as the “Program”).

children.label: A
children.text: to improve the competitiveness of United States manufacturing and to increase the production of goods manufactured predominantly within the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: to stimulate United States leadership in advanced manufacturing research, innovation, and technology;

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate the transition of innovative technologies into scalable, cost-effective, and high-performing manufacturing capabilities;

children.label: D
children.text: to facilitate access by manufacturing enterprises to capital-intensive infrastructure, including high-performance electronics and computing, and the supply chains that enable these technologies;

children.label: E
children.text: to accelerate the development of an advanced manufacturing workforce;

children.label: F
children.text: to facilitate peer exchange of and the documentation of best practices in addressing advanced manufacturing challenges;

children.label: G
children.text: to leverage non-Federal sources of support to promote a stable and sustainable business model without the need for long-term Federal funding;

children.label: H
children.text: to create and preserve jobs; and

children.label: I
children.text: to contribute to the development of regional innovation initiatives across the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the Manufacturing USA Network established under subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of Manufacturing USA institutes.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 4 Director
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the Program through the Director.

children.heading: Establishment of Manufacturing USA Network
children.label: c Establishment of Manufacturing USA Network

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As part of the Program, the Secretary shall establish a network of Manufacturing USA institutes.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 2 Designation
children.text: The network established under paragraph (1) shall be known as the “Manufacturing United States of America Network” or the “Manufacturing USA Network” (referred to in this section as the “Network”).

children.label: A
children.text: has been established by a person or group of persons to address challenges in advanced manufacturing and to assist manufacturers in retaining or expanding industrial production and jobs in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: has a predominant focus on a manufacturing process, novel material, enabling technology, supply chain integration methodology, or another relevant aspect of advanced manufacturing, such as nanotechnology applications, advanced ceramics, photonics and optics, composites, biobased and advanced materials, flexible hybrid technologies, tool development for microelectronics, food manufacturing, superconductors, advanced battery technologies, robotics, advanced sensors, quantum information science, supply chain water optimization, aeronautics and advanced materials, and graphene and graphene commercialization;

children.label: i
children.text: to improve the competitiveness of United States manufacturing, including key advanced manufacturing technologies such as nanotechnology, advanced ceramics, photonics and optics, composites, biobased and advanced materials, flexible hybrid technologies, tool development for microelectronics, food manufacturing, superconductors, advanced battery technologies, robotics, advanced sensors, quantum information science, supply chain water optimization, aeronautics and advanced materials, and graphene and graphene commercialization;

children.label: ii
children.text: to accelerate non-Federal investment in advanced manufacturing production capacity in the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to enable the commercial application of new technologies or industry-wide manufacturing processes; and

children.label: D
children.text: includes active participation among representatives from multiple industrial entities, research universities, community colleges, and other entities as appropriate, which may include industry-led consortia, career and technical education schools, Federal laboratories, State, local, and Tribal governments, businesses, educational institutions, and nonprofit organizations.

children.label: i
children.text: Research, development, and demonstration projects, including proof-of-concept development and prototyping, to reduce the cost, time, or risk of commercializing new technologies and improvements in existing technologies, processes, products, and research and development of materials to solve precompetitive industrial problems with economic or national security implications.

children.label: ii
children.text: Development and implementation of education, training, and workforce recruitment courses, materials, and programs addressing workforce needs through training and education programs at all appropriate education levels, including programs on applied engineering.

children.label: iii
children.text: Development of innovative methodologies and practices for supply chain integration and introduction of new technologies into supply chains, as appropriate.

children.label: iv
children.text: Outreach and engagement with small and medium-sized manufacturing enterprises, including women, minority, and veteran owned manufacturing enterprises, in addition to large manufacturing enterprises.

children.label: v
children.text: Development of roadmaps or leveraging of existing roadmaps with respect to technology areas being pursued by that Manufacturing USA institute that take into account the research and development undertaken at other Manufacturing USA institutes and Federal agencies with respect to such areas.

children.heading: Permissible activities
children.label: B Permissible activities
children.text: In addition to the activities set forth under subparagraph (A), a Manufacturing USA institute may carry out such other activities as may be consistent with the purposes set forth under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), the National Additive Manufacturing Innovation Institute and other manufacturing institutes formally recognized as Manufacturing USA institutes pursuant to Federal law or executive actions, or under pending interagency review for such recognition as of , shall be considered Manufacturing USA institutes for purposes of this section.December 16, 2014

children.heading: Network participation
children.label: B Network participation
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), an institute that is substantially similar to an institute described by paragraphs (1) and (2) but does not meet every element of such description and does not receive financial assistance under subsection (e) may, upon request of the institute, be recognized as a Manufacturing USA institute by the Secretary for purposes of participation in the Network.

children.label: I
children.text: is, as of , considered a Manufacturing USA institute under subparagraph (A) or recognized as a Manufacturing USA institute under subparagraph (B); andDecember 20, 2019

children.label: II
children.text: as of , receives Federal financial assistance under subsection (e) or otherwise consistent with the purposes of this section;December 20, 2019

children.label: ii
children.text: is under pending agency review for such recognition as of ; orDecember 20, 2019

children.label: iii
children.text: is currently funded by the Department of Energy.

children.heading: Financial assistance to establish and support Manufacturing USA institutes
children.label: e Financial assistance to establish and support Manufacturing USA institutes

children.heading: Financial assistance authorized
children.label: 1 Financial assistance authorized
children.text: Under the Program, the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy shall, and every other agency head may, award financial assistance to a person or group of persons to assist the person or group of persons in planning, establishing, or supporting a Manufacturing USA institute.

children.heading: Initial periods
children.label: A Initial periods
children.text: An award of financial assistance under paragraph (1) shall be awarded for an initial period of not less than 5 years and not more than 7 years.

children.heading: Renewal authorized
children.label: i Renewal authorized
children.text: An award of financial assistance under paragraph (1) may be renewed for additional periods, with each period not to exceed the duration of the initial period of the award, subject to a rigorous merit review.

children.heading: Consideration of performance standards
children.label: ii Consideration of performance standards
children.text: In carrying out a rigorous merit review under clause (i) for renewal of an award under such clause for a Manufacturing USA institute, an agency head shall consider the extent to which the institute has made progress in meeting the standards of performance established pursuant to paragraph (5)(C).

children.heading: Initial failure to meet performance standards
children.label: iii Initial failure to meet performance standards
I
children.text: notify the institute of any deficiencies in the performance of the institute; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide the institute one year to remedy such deficiencies.

children.heading: Further failure to meet performance standards
children.label: iv Further failure to meet performance standards
I
children.text: the institute shall not be eligible for renewed award under clause (i); and1So in original. Probably should be followed by “a”.1

children.label: II
children.text: the agency head that conducted the review for renewal shall notify the institute of such ineligibility.

children.heading: Continuation of existing Manufacturing USA institutes
children.label: v Continuation of existing Manufacturing USA institutes
I
children.text: may continue to receive support for the duration of the original funding award beginning on the date of establishment of that institute; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall be eligible for renewal of that funding pursuant to clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A person or group of persons seeking financial assistance under paragraph (1) shall submit to an agency head an application therefor at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the agency head may require.

children.label: i
children.text: A description of the specific sources and amounts of non-Federal financial support for the institute on the date financial assistance is sought.

children.label: ii
children.text: A description of the anticipated sources and amounts of non-Federal financial support during the period for which the institute could be eligible for continued Federal financial assistance under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: use a competitive, merit review process that includes review by a diverse group of individuals with relevant expertise from both the private and public sectors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that the technology focus of a Manufacturing USA institute does not substantially duplicate the technology focus of any other Manufacturing USA institute.

children.heading: Prohibition on participation by political appointees
children.label: i Prohibition on participation by political appointees
children.text: The review required by subparagraph (A)(i) may not include a review by a group of individuals that includes a political appointee.

children.label: I
children.text: ensures public transparency and accountability in the process used under subparagraph (A)(i); and

children.label: II
children.text: requires full disclosure of any real or potential conflicts of interest on the parts of individuals that participate in the process used under subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Definition of political appointee
children.label: iii Definition of political appointee
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “political appointee” has the meaning given such term in .section 714(h) of title 38

children.label: i
children.text: The potential of the Manufacturing USA institute to advance domestic manufacturing and the likelihood of economic impact, including the creation or preservation of jobs, in the predominant focus areas of the institute.

children.label: ii
children.text: The commitment of continued financial support, advice, participation, and other contributions from non-Federal sources, to provide leverage and resources to promote a stable and sustainable business model.

children.label: iii
children.text: Whether the financial support provided to the Manufacturing USA institute from non-Federal sources exceeds the requested Federal financial assistance.

children.label: iv
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will increase the non-Federal investment in advanced manufacturing research in the United States.

children.label: v
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will engage with small and medium-sized manufacturing enterprises to improve the capacity of such enterprises to commercialize new processes and technologies and to improve the domestic supply chain.

children.label: vi
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will carry out educational and workforce activities that meet industrial needs related to the predominant focus areas of the institute.

children.label: vii
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will advance economic competitiveness and generate substantial benefits to the Nation that extend beyond the direct return to participants in the Program.

children.label: viii
children.text: Whether the predominant focus of the Manufacturing USA institute is a manufacturing process, novel material, enabling technology, supply chain integration methodology, or other relevant aspect of advanced manufacturing that has not already been commercialized, marketed, distributed, or sold by another entity.

children.label: ix
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will strengthen and leverage the industrial, research, entrepreneurship, and other assets of a region.

children.label: x
children.text: How the Manufacturing USA institute will encourage the education and training of veterans and individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: develop metrics to assess the effectiveness of the activities funded in making progress toward the purposes of the Program set forth under subsection (b)(2), including the effectiveness of Manufacturing USA institutes in advancing technology readiness levels or manufacturing readiness levels;

children.label: B
children.text: establish standards for the performance of Manufacturing USA institutes that are based on the metrics developed under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: for each Manufacturing USA institute supported by the award, 5 years after the initial award and every 5 years thereafter until Federal financial assistance under this subsection is discontinued, conduct an assessment of the institute to confirm whether the performance of the institute is meeting the standards for performance established under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Collaboration
children.label: 6 Collaboration
children.text: In awarding financial assistance under paragraph (1), an agency head, in coordination with the National Program Office, as the agency head considers appropriate, may collaborate with Federal departments and agencies whose missions contribute to or are affected by advanced manufacturing, including, as the agency head considers appropriate, the Department of Agriculture, the Department of Defense, the Department of Education, the Department of Energy, the Department of Labor, the Food and Drug Administration, the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, the National Institutes of Health, and the National Science Foundation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an agency head may not, with respect to a Manufacturing USA institute, award financial assistance under paragraph (1) or renew an award of financial assistance under paragraph (2) unless the agency head determines that non-Federal funding comprises 50 percent or more of the total amount of funding made available for the operation and support of the institute.

children.heading: Waivers
children.label: B Waivers
children.text: An agency head awarding financial assistance under paragraph (1) with respect to a Manufacturing USA institute may waive the requirements of subparagraph (A) in the case of satellite centers, large capital facilities, equipment purchases, workforce development, or general operations.

children.label: A
children.text: contribute to the geographic diversity of the Manufacturing USA Program;

children.label: B
children.text: are located in an area with a low per capita income;

children.label: C
children.text: are located in an area with a high proportion of socially disadvantaged residents; or

children.label: D
children.text: are located in small and rural communities.

children.heading: Authority to award financial assistance for construction of test beds and specialized facilities
children.label: f Authority to award financial assistance for construction of test beds and specialized facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may, acting through the Director, award financial assistance for the construction of test beds and specialized facilities by Manufacturing USA institutes established or supported under subsection (e) as the Secretary considers appropriate to carry out the purposes of the Program.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall exercise authority under paragraph (1) in a manner and with requirements consistent with paragraphs (3) through (8) of subsection (e).

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 3 Priority
children.text: The Secretary shall establish preferences in selection criteria for proposals for financial assistance under this subsection from Manufacturing USA institutes that integrate as active members one or more covered entities as described in .section 18971 of title 42

children.heading: Grant program for public service activities for Manufacturing USA institutes without Federal funding
children.label: g Grant program for public service activities for Manufacturing USA institutes without Federal funding

children.text: are determined by the Secretary to be in the national interest; and
are unlikely to receive private sector financial support.

children.label: A
children.text: For each of the fiscal years 2015 through 2019, an amount not to exceed $5,000,000.

children.label: B
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2020 through 2030, such amounts as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: $70,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020, 2021, and 2022.

children.label: B
children.text: $84,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 and 2024.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, within the Institute, the National Office of the Manufacturing USA Network (referred to in this section as the “National Program Office”), which shall oversee and carry out the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: to oversee the planning, management, and coordination of the Program;

children.label: B
children.text: to coordinate with and, as appropriate, enter into memorandums of understanding with Federal departments and agencies whose missions contribute to or are affected by advanced manufacturing, including the Department of Agriculture, the Department of Defense, the Department of Education, the Department of Energy, the Department of Labor, the Food and Drug Administration, the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, the National Institutes of Health, and the National Science Foundation, to carry out the purposes set forth under subsection (b)(2);

children.label: C
children.text: to develop, not later than , a strategic plan to guide the Program, including a strategy for retaining domestic public benefits from Manufacturing USA institutes once Federal funding has been discontinued;December 16, 2015

children.label: D
children.text: to update the strategic plan developed under subparagraph (C) not less frequently than once every four years such that the planning cycle for each such update aligns with the planning cycle for updates to the strategic plan required under to better ensure the Program reflects the priorities of such strategic plan under such ;section 6622(c)(4) of title 42section 6622(c)(4) of title 42

children.label: E
children.text: to establish such procedures, processes, and criteria as may be necessary and appropriate to maximize cooperation and coordinate the activities of the Program with programs and activities of other Federal departments and agencies whose missions contribute to or are affected by advanced manufacturing;

children.label: F
children.text: to establish a clearinghouse of public information related to the activities of the Program;

children.label: G
children.text: to act as a convener of the Network;

children.label: H
children.text: to work with Federal agencies that are not sponsoring or supporting a Manufacturing USA institute to explore and develop options for sponsoring or supporting a Manufacturing USA institute;

children.label: I
children.text: to work with Federal agencies that are sponsoring or supporting a Manufacturing USA institute to develop and implement network-wide performance goals with measurable targets and timelines;

children.label: J
children.text: to help develop pilot programs that may be implemented by the Manufacturing USA institutes to address specific purposes of the Program, including to accelerate technology transfer to the private sector and to develop entrepreneurship programs;

children.label: K
children.text: to provide support services to promote workforce development activities, including the development of industry credentials;

children.label: L
children.text: to identify and disseminate best practices for workforce education and training across the Network and further enhance collaboration among Manufacturing USA institutes in developing and implementing such practices;

children.label: M
children.text: to collaborate with the Department of Labor, the Department of Education, industry, career and technical education schools, local community colleges, universities, and labor organizations to provide input, as appropriate, for the development of national certifications for advanced manufacturing workforce skills in the technology areas of the Manufacturing USA institutes; and

children.label: N
children.text: to coordinate with Manufacturing USA institutes to develop best practices for the membership agreements and coordination of similar project solicitations.

children.heading: Recommendations
children.label: 3 Recommendations
children.text: In developing the strategic plan under subparagraph (C) of paragraph (2) and updating such plan under subparagraph (D) of such paragraph, the Secretary shall solicit recommendations and advice from a wide range of stakeholders, including industry, small and medium-sized manufacturing enterprises, research universities, community colleges, State, Tribal, and local governments, and other relevant organizations and institutions on an ongoing basis.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 4 Report to Congress
children.text: Upon completion, the Secretary shall transmit the strategic plan required under paragraph (2)(C) to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives. Upon completion of each update required under paragraph (2)(D), the Secretary shall transmit such strategic plan to such committees.

children.label: i
children.text: significant outreach to, participation of, and engagement of small- and medium-sized manufacturers in Manufacturing USA institutes across the entirety of the manufacturing supply chain; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that the results of the Program, including technologies developed by the Program, reach small- and medium-sized manufacturers and that such entities have access to technical assistance, as appropriate, in deploying those technologies.

children.label: i
children.text: Support services for small- and medium-sized manufacturers, that many include the designation of a liaison.

children.label: ii
children.text: Assistance with workforce development.

children.label: iii
children.text: Technology transfer for small and medium-sized manufacturers.

children.label: iv
children.text: Such other areas as the Secretary determines appropriate to support the purposes of the Program.

children.heading: Detailees
children.label: 6 Detailees
children.text: Any Federal Government employee may be detailed to the National Program Office without reimbursement. Such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status or privilege.

children.heading: Council
children.label: A Council
children.text: The National Program Office shall establish or designate a council of heads of any Manufacturing USA institute receiving Federal funding at any time to foster collaboration between Manufacturing USA institutes.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: B Meetings
children.text: The council established or designated pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall meet not less frequently than twice each year.

children.heading: Duties of the council
children.label: C Duties of the council
children.text: The council established pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall assist the National Program Office in carrying out the functions of the National Program Office under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: require each recipient of such financial assistance submit to the agency head a report that describes the finances and performance of the Manufacturing USA institute with respect to which the financial assistance is awarded; and

children.label: ii
children.text: submit to the Secretary each report received by the agency head under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: an accounting of expenditures of amounts awarded to the recipient under subsection (e); and

children.label: I
children.text: its goals, plans, financial support, and accomplishments; and

children.label: II
children.text: how the Manufacturing USA institute has furthered the purposes set forth under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not less frequently than once each year until , the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress that describes the performance of the Program during the most recent 1-year period.December 31, 2030

children.label: i
children.text: a summary and assessment of the reports received by the Secretary under paragraph (1);

children.label: ii
children.text: an accounting of the funds expended by the Secretary under the Program, including any waivers made under subsection (e)(7)(B);

children.label: iii
children.text: an assessment of the participation in, and contributions to, the Network by any Manufacturing USA institutes not receiving financial assistance under subsection (e); and

children.label: iv
children.text: an assessment of the Program with respect to meeting the purposes set forth under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Assessments by Comptroller General of the United States
children.label: 3 Assessments by Comptroller General of the United States

children.heading: Assessments
children.label: A Assessments
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 3 years, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to Congress an assessment of the operation of the Program during the most recent 3-year period, including an assessment of the progress made towards achieving the goals specified in the national strategic plan for advanced manufacturing required under .section 6622(b)(7) of title 42

children.label: i
children.text: a review of the management, coordination, and industry utility of the Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of the extent to which the Program has furthered the purposes set forth under subsection (b)(2);

children.label: iii
children.text: such recommendations for legislative and administrative action as the Comptroller General considers appropriate to improve the Program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: an assessment as to whether any prior recommendations for improvement made by the Comptroller General have been implemented or adopted.

children.heading: Final assessment
children.label: C Final assessment
children.text: No later than , the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress a final report regarding the overall success of the Program.December 31, 2030

children.heading: Appointment of personnel and contracts
children.label: 1 Appointment of personnel and contracts
children.text: The Secretary may appoint such personnel and enter into such contracts, financial assistance agreements, and other agreements as the Secretary considers necessary or appropriate to carry out the Program, including support for research and development activities involving a Manufacturing USA institute.

children.heading: Transfer of funds
children.label: 2 Transfer of funds
children.text: Of amounts available under the authority provided by subsection (g), the Secretary may transfer to other Federal agencies such sums as the Secretary considers necessary or appropriate to carry out the Program. No funds so transferred may be used to reimburse or otherwise pay for the costs of financial assistance incurred or commitments of financial assistance made prior to .December 16, 2014

children.heading: Authority of other agencies
children.label: 3 Authority of other agencies
children.text: In the event that the Secretary exercises the authority to transfer funds to another agency under paragraph (2), such agency may accept such funds to award and administer, under the same conditions and constraints applicable to the Secretary, all aspects of financial assistance awards under this section.

children.heading: Use of resources
children.label: 4 Use of resources
children.text: In furtherance of the purposes of the Program, the Secretary may use, with the consent of a covered entity and with or without reimbursement, the land, services, equipment, personnel, and facilities of such covered entity.

children.heading: Acceptance of resources
children.label: 5 Acceptance of resources
children.text: In addition to amounts appropriated to carry out the Program, the Secretary may accept funds, services, equipment, personnel, and facilities from any covered entity to carry out the Program, subject to the same conditions and constraints otherwise applicable to the Secretary under this section and such funds may only be obligated to the extent provided for in advance by appropriations Acts.

children.heading: Covered entities
children.label: 6 Covered entities
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a covered entity is any Federal department, Federal agency, instrumentality of the United States, State, local government, Tribal government, territory, or possession of the United States, or of any political subdivision thereof, or international organization, or any public or private entity or individual.

children.label: A
children.text: at the Department of Labor relating to labor and apprenticeships;

children.label: B
children.text: at the Economic Development Administration relating to regional innovation, such as the Regional Innovation Strategies program;

children.label: C
children.text: at the Department of Education relating to workforce development, education, training, and retraining;

children.label: D
children.text: at the Department of Defense relating to procurement and other authorities of the Department of Defense;

children.label: E
children.text: at the Food and Drug Administration relating to biopharmaceutical manufacturing;

children.label: F
children.text: at the National Science Foundation, including the Advanced Technological Education program;

children.label: G
children.text: at the National Aeronautics and Space Administration relating to procurement, workforce development, education, training, and retraining;

children.label: H
children.text: at the Department of Energy relating to development of clean energy technologies and other authorities of the Department of Energy;

children.label: I
children.text: at the Department of Agriculture relating to outreach to rural communities;

children.label: J
children.text: additional programs that the Secretary determines are appropriate to support the activities of existing Manufacturing USA institutes; and2So in original. Subpars. (J) and (K) are identical and the words “additional programs” do not follow from introductory provisions.2

children.label: K
children.text: additional programs that the Secretary determines are appropriate to support the activities of existing Manufacturing USA institutes.2

children.heading: Patents
children.label: l Patents
children.text: Chapter 18 of title 35 shall apply to any funding agreement (as defined in section 201 of that title) awarded to new or existing Manufacturing USA institutes with respect to which financial assistance is awarded under subsection (e).

children.heading: References to prior names and terminology
children.label: m References to prior names and terminology
children.text: Any reference in law, regulation, map, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the “Network for Manufacturing Innovation Program”, the “Network for Manufacturing Innovation”, “National Office of the Network for Manufacturing Innovation Program”, or a “center for manufacturing innovation” shall be considered to be a reference to the Manufacturing USA Program, the Manufacturing USA Network, the National Office of the Manufacturing USA Network, or a Manufacturing USA institute, respectively.
